decisions.title: Prioritize Calculated Channels cache stability over feature expansion
decisions.context: Multiple cache-related bugs (ENG-10629, ENG-9807, ENG-10628) indicate architectural debt in calculated channels. This affects Nail the Core (Explore V2) and customer trust in decisions captured.

arguments.for: Cache bugs directly undermine decision quality. Three separate issues suggest systemic problem requiring architectural fix, not point solutions. Aligns with Signal>Evidence>Decision if users can't trust data freshness.
arguments.against: Calculated channels are already shipped. Customer-facing features (Canvas comments DES-164, Family alignment ENG-7789) may have higher visibility impact. Could address incrementally vs stopping feature work.

decisions.title: Accelerate Sift Edge (ENG-10376, ENG-10618) to production before EOQ2
decisions.context: Edge GUI search and log verbosity fixes suggest Edge is in late-stage hardening. Build the Moat strategy depends on Edge deployment at customer sites. Current trajectory unclear for Q2 revenue recognition.
decisions.blockedBy: Unclear production readiness criteria, security review status (ENG-10617 KMS re-encryption may be dependency)

arguments.for: Edge is differentiated moat technology. GUI search indicates customer-facing readiness. Log verbosity suggests performance tuning phase. Q2 deployment enables Q3 expansion revenue.
arguments.against: Forcing timeline risks quality issues in customer environments. PyRunner stuck state (ENG-10619) suggests core stability concerns. Better to nail reliability than hit arbitrary date.

decisions.title: Resource Canvas development (DES-164 comments) vs Python Rules hardening
decisions.context: Canvas comments/history (DES-164) is design work for Embed Deeper pillar. Python Rules has production issues (ENG-10619 PyRunner stuck state). Both affect decisions captured but different customer segments.

arguments.for: Canvas collaboration features directly increase decisions captured per customer by enabling team workflows. Comments are table-stakes for decision tools. Design work can parallel eng fixes.
arguments.against: PyRunner stuck states break existing customer workflows - retention risk. Python Rules is differentiated capability. Canvas without reliability is premature. Fix core before expanding surface area.

decisions.title: Commit to TRMC IDIQ sole-source strategy (CAGR-24) for test-center funding unlock
decisions.context: TRMC IDIQ represents Build the Moat opportunity in defense/test sector. Sole-source strategy requires legal/partnership investment. Modern Day Marine timing (CAGR-22) suggests near-term event forcing function.

arguments.for: Test center funding unlocks new customer category aligned with Edge strategy. Sole-source reduces sales cycle friction. DMV Tech Week + Modern Day Marine provide launch platform. Starpath MSA (CAGR-37) shows aerospace traction model.
arguments.against: Sole-source contracts require significant legal/compliance overhead. Distracts from core commercial SaaS motion. Test centers may have long procurement cycles undermining 2026 revenue goals. Resource intensive for unproven segment.

decisions.title: Sunset Family data volume limits (ENG-9568) or enforce with guardrails
decisions.context: Family feature enables multi-run analysis (Embed Deeper). Volume limits ticket suggests scaling concerns. Multiple Family UX tickets (ENG-8213, ENG-7789) indicate active development. Need stance on limits vs optimization.

arguments.for: Enforcing limits now prevents future scaling crisis and sets customer expectations. Aligns with sustainable growth. Allows engineering to focus on features vs performance firefighting.
arguments.against: Limits constrain power users who drive decisions captured metric. Competitive tools may not have limits. Better to optimize infrastructure (ENG-10623 bulk inserts) than restrict customers. Premature optimization.

today.breakdown: Stand-up (30m), Edge readiness review (90m), Canvas UX sync (60m), 1:1 Eng Lead (30m), 1:1 Design (30m), 1:1 CRO (30m), Strategy review (90m)

thisWeek.breakdown: Daily standups (2.5h), Pillar reviews (4.5h), Customer calls (3h), 1:1s (6h), Planning sessions (8h), All-hands (1h), TRMC strategy workshop (2h), Design critiques (3h), Incident review (2h)

timeAudit.weekSummary: 80% meeting load indicates decision bottleneck. Only 8 free hours for deep work on prioritization decisions (calculated channels vs Canvas vs Edge). Three open architecture decisions (cache, PyRunner, Family limits) require focused analysis time not available in current calendar. 1:1 load (9 total) suggests org scaling stress.
alerts: Critical: 4 P0 calculated channels bugs with no decision on architectural fix vs point solutions
Edge production deadline unclear despite active GUI/logging work - need forcing function decision by 4/25
TRMC sole-source decision (CAGR-24) requires legal review lead time - 5/1 deadline may slip without immediate action
Design capacity split across 3 major efforts (Canvas DES-164, Context Menu DES-151, Family UX) - prioritization needed
Meeting load unsustainable for product strategy role - recommend delegate pillar reviews or consolidate planning sessions
